content: Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

Gilen: Fuckin' hell, Shadr, this noon sun's beatin' down like Dibella herself is eye-fuckin' the whole damn plaza—clear as a virgin's conscience. You muckin' about with them horses again, or finally payin' off that Sapphire bitch's grip on your balls?

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): From MJR's vantage point on the weathered wooden bridge, the night sky overhead dances with swirling ribbons of emerald, violet, and fiery orange aurora, casting an ethereal glow over the surrounding landscape. Ahead, a sturdy timber-framed longhouse looms prominently, its dark walls etched with age and frost, while warm golden light spills from three rectangular windows on the upper floor, hinting at life within. To the sides, clusters of similar thatched-roof buildings huddle together, their silhouettes blending into the shadowy outskirts of what appears to be a remote settlement. Closer by, two figures stand on the creaking planks of the bridge: one slightly ahead, a burly man in fur-trimmed leather armor, his face partially obscured by a hooded cloak as he turns his head in conversation; the other lagging a step behind, similarly attired with a sword at his hip, gesturing animatedly with one hand. The air feels crisp and still, broken only by the faint groan of the bridge underfoot and the distant murmur of wind through the eaves.**
Shadr: Aye, Gilen, sun's got me sweatin' like a Thalmor spy in a Dark Elf bathhouse. Just finished groomin' the nags at the stables—Marise says they smell better than my debts now.

Shadr: Aye, that elf cunt Sapphire's off my back now—thanks to some nosy fucker settlin' the score. But look at this sky, Gilen, aurora twistin' like Dibella's wet dreams over these frostbitten shithouses. What the hell we doin' on this creakin' bridge in the dead of night, miles from Riften's stink? You peddlin' ghost wares to these backwoods pricks?

Gilen: Ha, debts'll choke ya tighter than a skeever in heat, but look at this fuckin' sky now—aurora's dancin' like Dibella's tits on feast night, paintin' the canal all green and purple. You and Marise plannin' to stargaze from the stables, or what? That longhouse over there's spillin' light like it's beggin' for trouble.

Gilen: Peddlin'? Ha, more like chasin' whispers of a fat score in this frost-rimed dump—bandits hoardin' Dwemer trinkets in that glowin' longhouse yonder. But fuck me, Shadr, this aurora's paintin' the whole shithole like Dibella's fever dream;

(The location is now Riften.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Sapphire, Shadr, Daegon, Lydia, Jenassa) (MJR: ...; Maul: I don't know you. You in Riften lookin' for trouble? MJR: Just passing through.; Maul: Yeah? Well, I got news for you; there's nothing to see here. Last thing the Black-Briars need is some stranger stickin' their nose where it doesn't belong. MJR: Who are the Black-Briars?; Maul: The Black-Briars have Riften in their pocket and the Thieves Guild watchin' their back, so keep your nose out of their business. Me? I'm Maul. I watch the streets for 'em. If you need dirt on anythin', I'm your guy... but it'll cost you. MJR: Here, what can you tell me? (142 gold); Maul: Pleasure doin' business with you. So what do you want to know? MJR: Any news involving the Dark Brotherhood?; Maul: The only thing I heard is a rumor that a boy named Aventus Aretino, in Windhelm, has been attempting to contact the Dark Brotherhood. He used to live here in Riften at the Orphanage on the other side of town, but I think he ran away. Can't say I blame him. MJR: Tell me more about the Black-Briars.; Maul: Well, you got Maven, she pretty much runs the whole operation. She's got friends in high places if you know what I mean. She's also got ties to the Dark Brotherhood and the Thieves Guild, so basically no one can touch her. Just remember, if she tells you to do somethin'... you damn well better do exactly what she says. MJR: Know anything about the Thieves Guild?; Maul: You kiddin'? My brother Dirge works in their hideout. I used to run with them myself, but took a job with Maven after they started hittin' a rough patch. If you want to get in on that action, find Brynjolf in the marketplace. I'm sure he could use someone like you.)

Maul: Late night at the Bee and Barb, eh Gilen? Clear skies outside, but in here it's gettin' steamy with all these patrons eyein' the help. You slinkin' around for business or pleasure tonight? Gilen: Business as usual, Maul—though pleasure sneaks in when the moon's high like this. That welcome sign's got folks lingerin' longer than they should at this hour. Maven keepin' you busy on the docks, or did she send you to watch the shadows in here too?
Maul: Maven's got me patrollin' wherever she needs eyes, Gilen—docks by day, taverns like this when the night's got too many whispers. Welcome

Gilen: Look at that sky, Maul—aurora dancin' like Dibella's own veil over the docks. Rarely see it this vivid at one in the mornin', pulls the eye right to those two on the bridge. That elf in nothin' but scraps, and her companion armed to the teeth... you reckon they're headin' for trouble or just showin' off under the lights? Maul: Aye, the lights are puttin' on a show tonight, Gilen—clear as crystal, no clouds to spoil it. Those lasses down there? Elf's got curves that'd make Maven jealous, and the blonde looks like she could handle herself... or us. Maven'd want me keepin' an eye, but hell, might be worth a closer look if they're lingerin' by Lake Honrich.
(Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Lydia, Jenassa, Riften Guard, Daegon, Horse)
